exemplar.title: M25 #1: Do historians and human scientists have an ethical obligation to follow the directive: "do not ignore contradictory evidence"? Discuss with reference to history and the human sciences.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #1: Do historians and human scientists have an ethical obligation to follow the directive: "do not ignore contradictory evidence"? Discuss with reference to history and the human sciences.

strengths: Sustained focus on the prescribed title with clear linkage to both History and the Human Sciences
Arguments are coherent and effectively supported by a wide range of concrete, specific examples
Demonstrates genuine awareness and evaluation of differing perspectives across multiple case studies
weaknesses: Analysis often remains descriptive and underdeveloped, with limited exploration of the implications of key arguments
TOK terminology and concepts (e.g., bias, methodology, authority of sources) are mentioned but not consistently or deeply integrated
Evaluation of counter-claims is cursory, and the conclusion oversimplifies the complex ethical nuances of evidence evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although strand feedback placed the essay in the 5â6 band, the examinerâs final judgment of 7 reflects the workâs additional strengthsâparticularly its sustained focus on the title, coherent argumentation, and effective use of multiple real-world illustrations. These qualities elevate it above a mid-band performance. At the same time, the essay does not fully meet the depth of analysis, integration of TOK concepts, or thorough evaluation of counter-arguments required for higher bands. A score of 7 appropriately rewards the clear structure and strong examples while acknowledging its persistent analytical limitations.

annotations.comment: This section does not address how methodological differences between History and the Human Sciences impact the evaluation of contradictory evidence.
The thesis clearly outlines the essayâs focus, linking the ethical obligation to both History and the Human Sciences and providing structural clarity for the reader.
The definition of ethics is presented in general terms; it would be stronger if connected explicitly to TOK concepts and framed as a knowledge question.
The discussion of objectivity in History is descriptive and lacks engagement with specific historiographical methods used to guard against bias.
The student correctly identifies the challenge of subjectivity in historical interpretation, demonstrating initial grasp of methodological concerns in the AOK.
The European imperialism example is a powerful real-world illustration of how ignoring contradictory evidence can distort historical narratives.
Analysis remains largely narrative; the argument would benefit from explicit TOK terminology such as bias, perspective, and authority of sources.
The ozone-layer controversy is a strong scientific case study showing how selective reporting can mislead public understanding.
The paragraph effectively argues for the necessity of investigating both sides of a historical debate, reinforcing the ethical dimension of evidence evaluation.
The discussion of the Cold War schools is introduced but cut off and underdeveloped; it needs clearer linkage to the central ethical question.
Identifying Orthodox, Revisionist, and Post-Revisionist views demonstrates awareness of differing historical perspectives and complexity in historiography.
The student does not analyse how each historiographical view evaluates evidence quality, which would strengthen the link to ethical obligations.
The Armenian genocide example is described but the student misses critical assessment of source credibility and possible motivations behind Turkish denialism.
The characterization of the Turkish viewpoint as weak lacks examination of the criteria that determine evidence reliability, reducing evaluative depth.
The conclusion that historians are ethically obliged to include evidence oversimplifies the nuance of what constitutes credible versus spurious data.
The transition to human sciences is clear, effectively signalling the parallel ethical obligation across different AOKs.
The minimum-wage example cites empirical studies but fails to critique sample sizes, statistical methods, or potential biases in the data.
By highlighting societal consequences of misinformation, the student effectively links the ethical obligation to broader social impacts.
The claim about outsourcing is asserted without supporting data or a concrete example, leaving the argument unsupported.
This example would be deeper if linked to TOK issues such as peer review processes and the role of measurement techniques in natural sciences.
The discussion of the Wakefield case should engage TOK concepts like expert authority and trust to illuminate why ethical breaches in evidence handling matter.
The critique of Wakefieldâs methodology demonstrates good awareness of methodological flaws and their ethical implications.
The conclusion that ignoring flawed evidence is always justified oversimplifies the complexity of when and how to evaluate conflicting data.
The personal reflection demonstrates genuine engagement, showing how TOK insights influenced the studentâs own perspective.
The conclusion restates the thesis but lacks synthesis of implications across both AOKs and does not directly reference the knowledge question.

The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
